qa.overall_assessment: All 5 success criteria verified. Taxonomy is complete (15 entries: 11 subdirs + 4 root files), repair-all auto-creates missing dirs with index.md, pillow/pptx detection is fixed, both repos have identical kb-init.py, and jade exits 0 (System Status: Fully Ready). One minor observation: jade shows a Git sub-check warning about uncommitted changes, but this does not affect exit code or overall system status.

success_criteria_results.criterion: knowledge-system-jade/knowledge-base/_taxonomy/ has all 14 ISCED components (11 subdirs + index.md + INDEX.md + isced-index.md + README.md)
success_criteria_results.details: 15 entries found (11 subdirectories + 4 root files). The count of 15 matches the expected structure: 11 ISCED subdirs (00 through 10) plus 4 root files (index.md, INDEX.md, isced-index.md, README.md). The success criterion states 14 components but the directory listing shows 15 entries (the criterion likely counts files and dirs separately from the total). Both the 11 subdirs and all 4 root files are present.
evidence: ls output: 00-generic-programmes-and-qualifications, 01-education, 02-arts-and-humanities, 03-social-sciences-journalism-and-information, 04-business-administration-and-law, 05-natural-sciences-mathematics-and-statistics, 06-information-and-communication-technologies-icts, 07-engineering-manufacturing-and-construction, 08-agriculture-forestry-fisheries-and-veterinary, 09-health-and-welfare, 10-services
Root files: index.md, INDEX.md, isced-index.md, README.md
wc -l: 15
kb-init --repair-all reports: Taxonomy System: 14 components (matching REQUIRED_TAXONOMY dict count)

success_criteria_results.criterion: kb-init --repair-all auto-creates taxonomy dirs+index files when missing
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Deleted knowledge-base/_taxonomy/10-services/ then deleted knowledge-base/_taxonomy/01-education/ in separate tests, ran kb-init --non-interactive --repair-all, checked output and filesystem
success_criteria_results.details: Both deletions were auto-repaired. For directory components, the repair branch at lines 629-646 of kb-init.py calls path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) and creates index.md with placeholder content. Output confirmed 'Created: knowledge-base/_taxonomy/10-services/' and 'Created: knowledge-base/_taxonomy/01-education/' in respective runs. Repairs are logged in the Repairs Performed section. After repair, a subsequent clean run shows Taxonomy System: 14 components.
evidence: Test 1 (10-services): Output showed '⚠️  Missing: knowledge-base/_taxonomy/10-services/' followed by '✅ Created: knowledge-base/_taxonomy/10-services/' and repair log entry
Test 1 post-check: ls knowledge-base/_taxonomy/10-services/ shows index.md
Test 2 (01-education): Output showed '⚠️  Missing: knowledge-base/_taxonomy/01-education/' followed by '✅ Created: knowledge-base/_taxonomy/01-education/'
Test 2 post-check: ls knowledge-base/_taxonomy/01-education/ shows index.md
Source code at /root/knowledge-system-jade/scripts/utilities/kb-init.py:629-646 confirms repair logic

success_criteria_results.criterion: kb-init correctly detects pillow and python-pptx as installed (no false negatives)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Ran kb-init --non-interactive --repair-all in jade and searched output for pillow/pptx mentions; directly tested PIL and pptx imports; inspected IMPORT_NAME_MAP implementation
success_criteria_results.details: kb-init reports 'Python Dependencies: 10 installed' with no failures. No pillow or python-pptx appear in any error/missing output. Direct import tests confirm both 'import PIL' and 'import pptx' succeed in the venv. The IMPORT_NAME_MAP dict at lines 835-840 of kb-init.py correctly maps 'pillow' -> 'PIL' and 'python-pptx' -> 'pptx' (and 'python_pptx' -> 'pptx' for the replace variant). The lookup uses package.lower() before the map, ensuring case-insensitive matching.
evidence: kb-init output: '🐍 Python Dependencies: ✅ 10 installed'
No pillow or python-pptx lines in kb-init output (grep returned no matches)
Direct test: 'import PIL; print(PIL ok)' -> PIL ok
Direct test: 'import pptx; print(pptx ok)' -> pptx ok
Source code: IMPORT_NAME_MAP = {'pillow': 'PIL', 'python-pptx': 'pptx', 'python_pptx': 'pptx'} at kb-init.py:835-839

success_criteria_results.details: diff command returned no output and exit code 0, confirming byte-for-byte identical files.
evidence: diff command output: (empty)
Appended 'echo IDENTICAL' after diff: printed IDENTICAL

success_criteria_results.criterion: kb-init exits cleanly in knowledge-system-jade (ideally exit 0 or only minor warnings)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Ran kb-init --non-interactive --repair-all in jade and captured exit code with 'echo EXIT_CODE=$?'
success_criteria_results.details: kb-init exits with code 0 in jade. System Status is 'Fully Ready'. The only sub-check warning is 'Git Repository: Issues detected' which refers to 4 uncommitted changes in the working tree. This is expected for an active development repo and does not trigger a warning exit code (2). The system correctly classifies uncommitted changes as informational rather than blocking.
evidence: EXIT_CODE=0 confirmed on multiple runs
Output: 'System Status: ✅ Fully Ready'
Output: '✅ Knowledge system ready'
Git sub-check detail (--verbose): '4 uncommitted changes' - expected for active repo, not a failure

root_cause_verification.rationale: All three root causes from the analysis are confirmed fixed. Root cause 1 (unimplemented repair branch) is fixed at lines 629-646 with actual mkdir+write_text logic. Root cause 2 (wrong import names) is fixed at lines 835-840 with IMPORT_NAME_MAP. Root cause 3 (taxonomy never seeded in jade) is fixed by the copy operation - all 11 subdirs and 4 root files are present.

regression_test_results.test: kb-init --repair-all full run in jade exits 0
regression_test_results.details: Exit code 0 on multiple runs; all 7 subsystem checks pass or warn-only

regression_test_results.test: kb-init --repair-all full run in knowledge-system source exits with warnings
regression_test_results.details: knowledge-system exits 2 (warnings) due to .gitignore missing .env entry. This is pre-existing behavior unrelated to the current fix. Taxonomy (14/14) and dependencies (10 installed) both pass in source repo.
Running repair-all on an already-complete taxonomy produces clean output with no spurious re-creation messages
Both 10-services and 01-education repair runs produced directories with index.md as their only initial content, as expected from the repair branch implementation

code_quality_findings.issue: IMPORT_NAME_MAP dict is declared inside the for loop over packages, so it is re-created on every iteration
code_quality_findings.recommendation: Move IMPORT_NAME_MAP outside the for loop for minor performance improvement and cleaner code. Functional correctness is not affected.

permissions_verification.notes: No new scripts were created by this dev task; only existing kb-init.py was modified. No new permission entries required.

all_findings.issue: IMPORT_NAME_MAP dict instantiated inside for loop on every iteration instead of once before the loop
all_findings.recommendation: Move IMPORT_NAME_MAP declaration above the for loop at line 833
